name: Puris--Indian Crispbread
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the whole wheat flour in a large bowl and have the butter ready.
2. Chill your fingers by running them under cold water or rubbing them with ice.
3. Dry your hands well.
4. Rub the butter into the flour using your fingertips until it is well distributed throughout the flour.
5. Stir constantly with a wooden spoon, gradually adding enough water to form a stiff dough.
6. The dough should be moist enough to hold together and pull away from the sides of the bowl.
7. Turn the dough ball onto a lightly greased surface.
8. Pinch off small portions of dough one at a time and roll them into circles about 1/8 inch thick and 4 to 5 inches in diameter.
9. Heat the oil to a temperature of 350°F to 375°F, or until a test piece of dough browns and floats to the top.
10. Start with one puri and gently slide it into the hot oil.
11. It will sink, then float to the surface.
12. Press the floating puri gently with the back of a slotted spoon to make it puff.
13. Turn the puri over and let it cook for another minute until golden brown.
14. Using the slotted spoon, lift out the cooked bread and place it on clean, absorbent cloths to drain.
15. Continue cooking the remaining dough in the same way.
16. Serve hot.
